description_long: This object is a large, rectangular concrete air conditioner housing with a weathered, moss-stained exterior, commonly found in urban environments. The structure sits on a slightly raised concrete base and features a flat, rough-textured top surface. One side has a prominent black metal grille, likely serving as the air intake or exhaust, with horizontal slats and a recessed placement. The other sides are solid concrete, showing signs of age, discoloration, and some minor surface damage. The overall appearance suggests durability and resistance to outdoor conditions, with no visible control panel or branding. The object is robust and immovable, designed to protect internal air conditioning components in public or semi-public spaces.
description: A large, weathered concrete air conditioner housing with a black metal grille on one side, sitting on a raised base and showing signs of outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: The front-left view shows the rough concrete surface, moss stains, and the black metal grille partially visible on the right side.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the black metal grille with horizontal slats, set into the concrete face, and the mossy, aged texture around the edges.
description_view_2: The rear-left view displays a solid concrete wall with no openings, showing discoloration and some surface wear near the base.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals another solid concrete wall, with visible weathering and moss growth along the bottom edge.
attribute_grille_pattern: Horizontal metal slats, evenly spaced, forming a rectangular grille recessed into the concrete face.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Single rectangular vent with horizontal slats, located on the front face, recessed and protected by a metal grille.
